source.definition: a place where drinks, especially alcoholic beverages, are served and consumed

explanation.sourceText: The English word "bar" comes from the Old French "barre", meaning a rod or barrier. It originally referred to the counter where drinks are served.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „bar” provine din francezul vechi „barre”, care înseamnă o tijă sau o barieră. Inițial desemna tejgheaua de unde se servesc băuturi.

source.additional: In many cities, there are special bars offering a wide variety of drinks.

target.definition: loc unde se servesc și consumă băuturi alcoolice sau răcoritoare

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word "bar" was directly borrowed from the French "barre" and denotes a place where drinks are served.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „bar” este preluat direct din francezul „barre” și desemnează un loc unde se servesc băuturi.

target.additional: Das Wort wird sowohl im Plural als auch im Singular verwendet.

examples.sourceText: I am going to the bar tonight.
examples.targetText: Merg în bar în această seară.

examples.sourceText: There are many cocktails in this bar.
examples.targetText: La acest bar sunt multe cocktailuri.

examples.sourceText: The bar has long opening hours.
examples.targetText: Barul are ore lungi de funcționare.

examples.sourceText: I ordered a drink at the bar.
examples.targetText: Am comandat o băutură la bar.

examples.sourceText: Many people meet at the bar.
examples.targetText: Mulți oameni se întâlnesc la bar.

learningTips.text: The English word "bar" sounds exactly like the Romanian "bar" – imagine sitting with friends enjoying drinks at a bar!
Remember: a bar is where you get "băutură" (drinks) – this helps you connect the word to its meaning!
Think of the phrase "one more at the bar" – it links the word to a typical social situation.
Connect "bar" to its French origin "barre" – think of the counter as a rod where you get your drink.
